spec_match.rationale: Concept exercises the relevant API surface (fetch(, window., SharedWorker) with runtime script (~12190 chars) wired to live controls, in line with the feature summary.

interactive.rationale: Real interactive surface — 6 buttons, 0 inputs, 0 selects, 0 ranges — wired up via event listeners.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (clients, connecting, disconnecting, events, keepalive, lifecycle, machine, shared, simulate, starting, state, terminated).

edge_cases.rationale: No visible edge case / fallback / error handling — only the happy path.

references.rationale: ChromeStatus link is present but the page has no dedicated `references` section — the self-heal contract is fragile.

design_system.rationale: All colours come via 69 CSS-variable references, no raw hex, no button background override without a colour override, and not a static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Add explicit edge case coverage
openQuestions.detail: Pick one failure mode the spec or chromestatus motivation calls out (unsupported browser, denied permission, empty input, etc.) and surface it as a distinct demo state alongside the happy path.

openQuestions.title: Wrap references in a labelled `references` section
openQuestions.detail: Per CLAUDE.md rule #3, the chromestatus link is the self-heal anchor and should sit inside a clearly-labelled `references` section so the recovery script can find it reliably.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("This update adds a new option, `extendedLifetime: true`, to the `SharedWorker` constructor. This new option requests that the shared worker be kept alive even after all current clients have unloaded. This allows pages to perform asynchronou") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://github.com/whatwg/html/commit/9c009049e4fa9dba638ef68ca502b781082bbb68 to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~12190 chars of script across 6 button(s) and 0 other control(s). hits 4 pass / 1 partial / 1 fail across the six rubric criteria.
